question_text: Two waves are compared. Wave A has a higher amplitude than Wave B. What does this mean?

options.label: A
options.text: Wave A vibrates more times per second than Wave B

options.label: B
options.text: Wave A has a longer distance between crests than Wave B

options.label: C
options.text: Wave A carries more energy than Wave B

options.label: D
options.text: Wave A travels faster than Wave B

correct_answer: Wave A carries more energy than Wave B
explanation: Amplitude is the maximum displacement of a wave from its rest position. A larger amplitude means the wave vibrates with greater intensity and carries more energy. This is a fundamental relationship in wave physics.
